title: Highest temperature in London on June 14?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 14 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the London City Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Market-implied odds favor a June 14 high of 21–22 °C in London, with combined probability above 65 %.** Official Met Office guidance issued on 13 June projects a maximum of 22 °C under mostly dry conditions with sunny intervals and light winds. This aligns with near-normal early-summer values following the exceptional May warmth; models show limited diurnal heating due to modest insolation and a stable air mass. Ensemble spreads and slight variations in cloud cover or boundary-layer mixing keep 20 °C and 23 °C viable but lower-probability outcomes. Traders are weighting the latest deterministic and ensemble runs from the Met Office and ECMWF, which currently cluster around these central values, while monitoring any 14 June morning updates for shifts in the forecast.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders see the 25°C and 26°C outcomes nearly tied because the latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble runs show maximum temperatures clustering around those values under building high pressure and mostly clear skies over Paris on June 14. Light winds and strong daytime insolation support modest warming above seasonal norms near 23–24°C, while slight differences in boundary-layer moisture and timing of any residual cloud cover keep the two bins competitive. Warmer 27°C+ outcomes remain possible if subsidence strengthens further, though current guidance favors the lower side of the range.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 14 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Incheon Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent numerical weather model runs from the Korea Meteorological Administration and global ensembles point to a moderate high-pressure ridge over the Korean Peninsula, favoring mostly clear to partly cloudy conditions and daytime highs clustered around 26–28°C for Seoul on June 14. This setup explains the tight market clustering, with 27°C holding a slight edge at 37.5% implied probability over 26°C at 33.5%. Minor uncertainties in boundary-layer mixing, surface heating rates, and any weak frontal moisture could shift the exact maximum by 1–2°C, keeping adjacent outcomes viable. Ensemble spreads remain narrow given the short lead time, consistent with typical early-June variability before stronger monsoon influences arrive later in the month.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models for Ankara on June 14 show a tight range centered near 21–22°C, driven by persistent cloud cover, isolated thunderstorms, and northerly winds limiting daytime heating. Official guidance from sources like the Turkish Meteorological Service and international models (ECMWF/GFS) highlights overcast conditions and light precipitation potential that cap maximum temperatures below seasonal averages around 25–28°C. Recent observations on June 13 indicate similar stabilization after earlier warmth, with surface data and ensemble spreads keeping probabilities balanced between the two leading outcomes. Resolution hinges on the official daily maximum recorded at Ankara’s primary station, where even modest shifts in timing or intensity of any showers could tip the result.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Northerly and northwesterly flows are the main driver positioning 17°C as the market favorite (59.5% implied probability) for Wellington’s June 14 maximum, followed by 18°C (26%) and 16°C (15%).** These outcomes reflect model consensus around a warmer-than-climatological day in early winter, when the long-term June average high sits near 12–13°C.

Recent MetService guidance and related short-range runs show northwesterlies sustaining mild advection, with afternoon peaks most likely in the mid-to-upper teens. This aligns with the early-June warm spell that produced record readings above 19°C and keeps current ensemble guidance elevated relative to seasonal norms. NIWA’s April–June outlook called for near-average temperatures overall, but the specific synoptic setup—mild northerly air mass with limited cloud or shower suppression—favors the 16–18°C cluster over cooler outcomes.

Traders appear to weigh the narrow spread of latest model solutions against residual uncertainty from shower timing or frontal passage, which could cap the high at 16°C or allow a brief push to 18°C. With resolution tied to official MetService observations the following day, the market-implied odds embed both the current forecast signal and the typical 1–2°C envelope of short-range temperature uncertainty in Wellington’s exposed, wind-influenced environment.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Market-implied odds favor 26°C (41.5%) and 27°C (39.0%) as the highest temperature in Tokyo on June 14, 2026, with 28°C a distant third at 11.2%.** 

Japan Meteorological Agency forecasts issued June 13 project a mostly cloudy day with a maximum of 28°C and minimum near 20°C, yet persistent cloud cover and scattered showers associated with the early tsuyu rainy season are likely to limit peak solar heating and keep readings closer to the 26–27°C range. Key differentiating factors include insolation duration, boundary-layer moisture, and any sea-breeze reinforcement from Tokyo Bay, which can suppress afternoon temperatures by 1–2°C under overcast conditions. Historical mid-June normals hover around 26°C, and recent model consensus shows limited warming potential without clearing skies. Updated JMA and ensemble guidance on the morning of June 14 will be the decisive data release for traders.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Trader sentiment for Hong Kong's June 14 maximum temperature centers on 29–30°C, reflecting official forecasts of heavy rain and persistent cloud cover that limit daytime heating.** The Hong Kong Observatory's outlook projects a 26–29°C range under unsettled conditions, with accumulated rainfall potentially reaching 200 mm over two days, consistent with seasonal monsoon influences and a trough pattern suppressing solar insolation. While the June–August seasonal forecast calls for above-normal temperatures overall, short-term model guidance points to a slightly cooler-than-climatological peak, aligning with recent daily highs near 28°C and the subtropical climate's typical June averages of 30–31°C. Heavy precipitation acts as the primary near-term catalyst, increasing confidence in outcomes below 31°C while leaving modest room for variance if rainfall timing shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for Singapore's June 14 maximum temperature centers on the southwest monsoon's influence, with official Meteorological Service forecasts calling for a 34°C peak amid ongoing early-June heat but thundery afternoon showers over northern, western, and central areas. These convective developments, driven by high humidity and typical equatorial instability, are expected to increase cloud cover and rainfall, moderating highs toward the 32°C mode that holds 49.5% implied probability. Recent model consensus and today's observed 34°C readings reinforce 31–33°C outcomes as most likely, while lower probabilities for 34°C or above reflect uncertainty in shower timing and intensity from numerical weather models. Historical June averages near 31°C provide baseline context for this pattern.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from major agencies show Milan under high pressure with southerly flow advecting warmer air into the Po Valley on June 14, supporting a daily maximum near 30–31 °C. Urban heat-island effects and limited mixing typical of the region add a few degrees relative to rural stations. Ensemble spreads remain narrow but allow for minor downward revisions if afternoon cloud cover increases or if boundary-layer winds strengthen slightly. Historical mid-June climatology averages 26–28 °C, so current guidance reflects a modest positive anomaly. The closely bunched market probabilities around 30–32 °C reflect this forecast consensus together with acknowledged uncertainty in exact peak timing and local microclimate influences ahead of final observational verification.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 14 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/es/madrid/LEMD.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from European and global centers indicate a stable high-pressure ridge over the Iberian Peninsula, promoting mostly clear skies, light northerly winds, and strong daytime solar insolation that favors a Madrid maximum near 33–34 °C on June 14. This aligns with the market’s heaviest implied probabilities on those two outcomes, reflecting the consensus range from recent model runs and climatological baselines where mid-June highs typically reach 28–32 °C but have trended warmer under current large-scale patterns. Minor spread in guidance arises from slight variations in boundary-layer mixing and any late-day convective development, keeping lower or higher thresholds at single-digit odds. Updated model cycles and official AEMET guidance ahead of the daily maximum will provide the final resolution data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast consensus from meteorological sources positions 16°C as the leading outcome for Warsaw’s June 14 high, with model guidance showing a cool, unsettled pattern featuring thundery showers and moderate westerly winds that suppress daytime warming. Recent runs indicate afternoon maxima near 16°C (61°F) under mostly cloudy skies, consistent with the 42.5% implied probability and closely followed by 17°C at 31.5%. Cooler 15°C scenarios gain traction if showers arrive earlier or are heavier than modeled, while brief clearing could allow a push toward 17–18°C. Historical early-June variability and the short lead time to resolution keep the distribution tight around these central values, with traders weighting official guidance and ensemble spreads accordingly.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent numerical weather prediction models and climatological data for mid-June in the Sichuan Basin position 33°C as the leading outcome for Chengdu’s daily maximum on June 14, with 34°C and 32°C close behind. Persistent high humidity from the advancing East Asian monsoon, combined with expected afternoon cloud cover and possible scattered thunderstorms, typically limits peak heating despite seasonal warming. Current forecasts from sources such as AccuWeather and regional models show highs clustering near 31–33°C under partly cloudy skies, consistent with historical mid-month averages of 29–31°C. Trader sentiment reflects this consensus while incorporating residual uncertainty from model spread on exact cloud timing and localized heating, keeping probabilities for extremes below 10%. Updated runs ahead of the event could shift implied odds if clearer or drier conditions develop.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Trader consensus centers on 32°C as the most probable peak temperature for Kuala Lumpur on June 14, 2026, reflecting the city’s typical June climatology and short-range forecast guidance.** 

Kuala Lumpur’s equatorial climate produces narrow temperature ranges year-round, with June average daily highs near 32°C and a historical distribution where 31–33°C accounts for the large majority of observations. Afternoon convective thunderstorms, driven by high humidity and daytime heating, frequently limit maximum temperatures by increasing cloud cover and evaporative cooling, which narrows the distribution around the mean. 

Current model runs and official agency guidance for the next 24–48 hours show highs in the 31–33°C range, with isolated to scattered thunderstorms expected during peak heating hours—conditions that have prevailed in recent days and align closely with the market’s implied probabilities. Earlier June readings reached 34–36°C under clearer skies, but the present pattern of moisture and instability favors values closer to the long-term June average. 

Upcoming NHC-style or Malaysian Meteorological Department updates on diurnal convection timing will be the primary near-term catalyst that could shift probabilities between the 31°C, 32°C, and 33°C buckets.

eventMetadata.context_description: Consensus short-range meteorological guidance positions 29°C as the leading outcome for Guangzhou’s June 14 high, reflecting expected daytime maxima near 28–30°C under a typical early-summer subtropical monsoon pattern. Persistent high humidity above 80 percent, combined with forecast afternoon thundery showers and increased cloud cover, limits surface heating and aligns with recent observations of highs in the upper 20s to low 30s. Model consensus from agencies such as the China Meteorological Administration and international ensembles shows limited potential for stronger insolation or clearer skies that would push readings to 31°C or above, keeping those probabilities low. Traders are monitoring the final 24-hour forecast updates and any shifts in steering flow or convective timing that could alter the daily maximum before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent model consensus from sources like timeanddate and AccuWeather points to a daily maximum near 23–26°C in Qingdao on June 14, 2026, driven by moderate onshore flow from the Yellow Sea and partly cloudy conditions that limit strong daytime heating. This coastal moderation, typical for mid-June in Shandong Province where climatological averages sit around 22–24°C, positions 26°C as the market leader at 47.5% implied probability while 25°C and 27°C capture the remaining spread from ensemble uncertainty. Earlier June warmth reaching 29–31°C has given way to cooler steering patterns, with no major warm advection or clear-sky amplification expected overnight into the 14th. Traders appear to weight the latest short-range guidance heavily, as any shift in boundary-layer moisture or wind direction could nudge the peak by 1–2°C before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Forecast models from sources like Weather25 indicate a likely daily high near 11°C for Buenos Aires on June 14, 2026, aligning with the market’s narrow lead for that outcome at 39% versus 37.5% for 10°C.**  

In mid-June, the city experiences typical winter conditions under the influence of the Río de la Plata estuary, where onshore flow and variable cloud cover can shift maximum temperatures by 1–2°C. Recent model runs show subtle differences in the timing of any weak frontal passage or high-pressure ridge, which directly affects whether daytime readings peak at 10°C or 11°C. Historical June highs average 14–16°C but frequently dip lower with cool southerly winds, creating genuine uncertainty that keeps the two leading outcomes nearly tied. Traders appear to weigh the latest short-range guidance against climatological baselines as resolution nears.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecast updates from Environment Canada and global models position Toronto’s June 14 maximum near 22–23 °C as the most probable outcome, with traders assigning roughly 58 % combined probability to these two bins. A cooler-than-average air mass and increased cloud cover associated with mid-month troughing have limited daytime heating, keeping surface temperatures below typical June climatology of 24–25 °C. Minor model spread on the exact timing of any weak frontal passage or afternoon clearing creates the tight contest between 22 °C and 23 °C, while probabilities fall sharply above 25 °C or below 21 °C. Updated model runs and official guidance expected later today will likely refine these margins ahead of market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Recent forecast guidance points to a high in the upper 80s to low 90s°F for Houston on June 14, 2026, driven by expected afternoon convection and increased cloud cover that will limit peak heating.** Official normals for mid-June place the average high near 92°F, but National Weather Service and private model consensus currently favor scattered thunderstorms developing after midday, with southeasterly flow and moderate humidity supporting widespread cloudiness rather than full sun. This setup typically caps temperatures several degrees below clear-sky potential in the Houston subtropical climate.

Leading market-implied probabilities cluster on 88–91°F ranges because updated short-range guidance shows the highest likelihood of measurable rain or thick cloud decks during the warmest hours, preventing the mercury from reaching or exceeding the seasonal normal. Lower-probability tails (above 93°F or below 86°F) reflect residual uncertainty in exact storm timing and coverage; a delayed or more isolated convective pattern could allow brief clearing and a higher reading, while earlier or heavier rain would favor the lower end. The next NWS forecast updates and morning model runs will provide the clearest signals ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Persistent June Gloom, driven by a strong marine layer and onshore flow, is the dominant factor capping daytime highs in Los Angeles for June 14. Official National Weather Service guidance and model consensus indicate morning low clouds and fog transitioning to partial afternoon clearing, limiting solar heating and keeping maximum temperatures near 72–75 °F at downtown and coastal stations. This aligns with recent observed cooling after early-June warmth, when highs briefly reached the mid-80s before the marine influence strengthened. Historical climatology shows mid-June coastal maxima averaging 74–77 °F under similar patterns, and current ensemble guidance shows limited warming potential absent a significant offshore wind shift. Traders are therefore concentrating probability on the 72–75 °F brackets, with only modest odds extending to 76 °F or higher if the marine layer erodes faster than expected.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Marine layer persistence and onshore flow are the main drivers keeping San Francisco’s June 14 high in the narrow 70–75 °F range that dominates current market pricing.** Official guidance from NOAA and NWS points to a classic early-summer pattern: a strong marine layer advecting cool, moist air from the Pacific, with fog and low stratus expected to linger into the morning and limit daytime heating. Model consensus favors a peak near 68–72 °F at the downtown or Mission District sensors, though slight variations in wind direction or a delayed marine push could allow brief clearing and an extra 2–3 °F of warming. Recent days have shown similar outcomes—June 12 settled at 70–71 °F—reflecting how sensitive the daily maximum remains to the timing of fog burn-off versus any weak offshore component. Traders therefore cluster probability across the three central bins rather than committing to a single narrow outcome.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecast models from sources like AccuWeather and timeanddate converge on a daily high of 23–26°C for Mexico City on June 14, driven by the city's high elevation near 2,240 meters, which typically moderates temperatures, combined with the onset of the rainy season featuring afternoon cloud cover and showers that cap daytime peaks. Historical June averages hover around 24–25°C, with low heat and moderate humidity, positioning 25°C and 26°C as the market leaders at 35.5% and 22.5% implied probability. Any shifts in steering winds or precipitation timing could alter the exact maximum recorded at official stations, with final data expected shortly after the date.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Marcos A. Gelabert Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pa/panama-city/MPMG.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Panama City be 32°C on June 14?

eventMetadata.context_description: **Traders see 30°C and 31°C as nearly equally likely for Panama City’s June 14 maximum because mid-June climatology centers on 29–31°C highs.** Equatorial solar insolation and warm surrounding sea-surface temperatures supply consistent boundary-layer heat and moisture, while the onset of the rainy season brings frequent afternoon convection that caps extremes through cloud shading and evaporative cooling. Subtle differences between the two outcomes hinge on forecast details such as exact timing of cloud build-up, boundary-layer mixing depth, and any shift in trade-wind strength or local sea-breeze convergence. Current model consensus and recent station observations show no strong deviation from seasonal norms, keeping the market tightly balanced between these two adjacent bins.
